title: hieracium lazistanum subsp. marotii
intro: Hieracium lazistanum subsp. marotii, also known as the Marot's hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, Marot's hawkweed is often used to make infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be applied topically to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
